title: ulmus parvifolia var. coreana
intro: Ulmus parvifolia var. coreana, also known as the Korean elm, is a variety of the Chinese elm tree native to Korea and China. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ulmus parvifolia var. coreana include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential in preventing cardiovascular disease and cancer. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the Korean elm tree are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
